2074248468647039186.headline: Disciplinary Committee
2074248468647039186.body: Statement from the Chairperson of the FIFA Disciplinary Committee on Flo Balogun's red. No specific reasons given for why the independent panel chose to suspend the red card other than discretion. Summary: - On 2 July 2026, FIFA opened disciplinary proceedings against Balogun for possible breaches of 66 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code (expulsion and match suspension related to the red card) and Article 14 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code (misconduct of players related to the celebration). - On 5 July 2026, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee found Balogun guilty of both infringements and imposed a one-match ban, suspended on probation for one year, and a $40,000 fine. - The FIFA Disciplinary Committee chose to apply Article 27 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code under which it has discretion to suspend the implementation of any disciplinary measures. - Balogun was handed a one-match suspended ban which remains dormant during the probationary period and will only be activated if he commits another infringement of a similar nature and gravity. - The FIFA Disciplinary Committee stress their independence. - The FIFA Disciplinary Committee add they did not reverse the referee’s on-field sending-off of Balogun. - Their sole logic is that under Article 27 of the FDC, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee has the discretion to suspend the implementation of any disciplinary measures so long as they do not relate to match manipulation... The exercise of such discretion is fully consistent with the general guiding principles for the determination of the applicable disciplinary sanction pursuant to Article 25 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
2074248468647039186.raw_text:
Statement from the Chairperson of the FIFA Disciplinary Committee on Flo Balogun's red. No specific reasons given for why the independent panel chose to suspend the red card other than "discretion".

Summary:
- On 2 July 2026, FIFA opened disciplinary proceedings against Balogun for possible breaches of 66 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code (expulsion and match suspension related to the red card) and Article 14 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code (misconduct of players related to the celebration).
- On 5 July 2026, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee found Balogun guilty of both infringements and imposed a one-match ban, suspended on probation for one year, and a $40,000 fine.
- The FIFA Disciplinary Committee chose to apply Article 27 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code "under which it has discretion to suspend the implementation of any disciplinary measures."
- Balogun was handed a one-match suspended ban which "remains dormant during the probationary period and will only be activated if he commits another infringement of a similar nature and gravity."
- The FIFA Disciplinary Committee stress their independence.
- The FIFA Disciplinary Committee add they "did not reverse the referee’s on-field sending-off of Balogun".
- Their sole logic is that "under Article 27 of the FDC, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee has the discretion to suspend the implementation of any disciplinary measures so long as they do not relate to match manipulation"... "The exercise of such discretion is fully consistent with the general guiding principles for the determination of the applicable disciplinary sanction pursuant to Article 25 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code."

2074222278745657410.headline: Joachim Watzke
2074222278745657410.body: 🚨🆕 DFB vice president Hans-Joachim Watzke confirms our news about Jürgen Klopp: “It’s not a done deal yet. There are still hurdles, mainly due to his contract with RB. 2074245791967748544.headline: Cristiano Ronaldo
2074245791967748544.body: The show is over. Cristiano Ronaldo’s 2026 World Cup dream ended the way it started, with serious questions around whether this was a tournament too far for a man who looked what he is: a 41-year-old trying to push back the hands of time. He became the first player to score in six World Cups, but there’s little else that will be recalled fondly about his presence in these finals. Two goals against Uzbekistan and a penalty against Croatia was as good as it got. He was peripheral against Spain and that feels like the story of his World Cup. 🔗 nyti.ms/4y6pQXl

The show is over.

Cristiano Ronaldo’s 2026 World Cup dream ended the way it started, with serious questions around whether this was a tournament too far for a man who looked what he is: a 41-year-old trying to push back the hands of time.

2074261148170666456.headline: Roberto Martínez
2074261148170666456.body: Roberto Martínez announces he is standing down as Portugal head coach. “It is true that this was my last match with the Portugal national team. But I have two things to say: “First of all, I want to thank the Portuguese people because this has been an incredible journey - an honour that I simply cannot put into words. The passion and energy of the fans, of the entire country… thank you for all of that. I will carry those memories with me for the rest of my life. “Secondly, I want to thank the players, because they have been incredible. There is an enormous amount of talent, but above all, there has been a commitment to becoming a team. Having great players in the national team is easy - they are the very best - but what truly matters is building a team. “There have been 45 matches, producing the best record in Portugal’s history. That has been possible because of the players’ commitment. I leave with unforgettable memories: winning the Nations League, breaking records… “I also want to express my deepest gratitude to the Federation for all the support they have given us, and to the coaching staff, who demonstrated an incredible level of professionalism from day one. We worked tirelessly to help our players. I leave with unforgettable memories and my most sincere thanks to the Portuguese people.”
2074261148170666456.raw_text:
Roberto Martínez announces he is standing down as Portugal head coach. “It is true that this was my last match with the Portugal national team. But I have two things to say:

“First of all, I want to thank the Portuguese people because this has been an incredible journey - an honour that I simply cannot put into words. The passion and energy of the fans, of the entire country… thank you for all of that. I will carry those memories with me for the rest of my life.

“Secondly, I want to thank the players, because they have been incredible. There is an enormous amount of talent, but above all, there has been a commitment to becoming a team. Having great players in the national team is easy - they are the very best - but what truly matters is building a team.

“There have been 45 matches, producing the best record in Portugal’s history. That has been possible because of the players’ commitment. I leave with unforgettable memories: winning the Nations League, breaking records…

“I also want to express my deepest gratitude to the Federation for all the support they have given us, and to the coaching staff, who demonstrated an incredible level of professionalism from day one. We worked tirelessly to help our players. I leave with unforgettable memories and my most sincere thanks to the Portuguese people.”

2074334569877372945.headline: Mauricio Pochettino
2074334569877372945.body: 🗣️ Mauricio Pochettino adds: In a personal way, what is the point to insult or to receive a lot of bad messages or threats? My position is, I am the head coach and the rules said it is possible for the federation to try and make the player available. My position was to train the team. If you have available Balogun because the disciplinary committee of FIFA allowed it, then it is not a problem. But in a personal way, I feel so dissappointed with too many people talking about politics and manipiation or ethics and integrity. It not an excuse to say we didn't perform because of that. That's not true. Ben Jacobs (JacobsBen) 🗣️ Mauricio Pochettino on whether the Flo Balogun saga was a distraction: I think today we just didn't show our real quality as a team. I want to congratulate Belgium because they were good. But I think we were not at our level. We started in a poor way. We never found the flow of the game. Even after a we scored, in the next action we conceded a goal. It was a very bad day. We didn't show the quality from previous games. We have to accept we weren't good. We don't need to find excuses. FIFA and all the different people involved explained, and those are the rules. It was a clear explanation, so I have no comment on that [President Trump admitting today he called Gianni Infantino to review Balogun's red]. Belgium was the better team. —
2074334569877372945.raw_text:
🗣️ Mauricio Pochettino adds: "In a personal way, what is the point to insult or to receive a lot of bad messages or threats? My position is, I am the head coach and the rules said it is possible for the federation to try and make the player available. My position was to train the team.

"If you have available Balogun because the disciplinary committee of FIFA allowed it, then it is not a problem.

"But in a personal way, I feel so dissappointed with too many people talking about politics and manipiation or ethics and integrity.

"It not an excuse to say we didn't perform because of that. That's not true."


Ben Jacobs (@JacobsBen)

🗣️ Mauricio Pochettino on whether the Flo Balogun saga was a distraction: "I think today we just didn't show our real quality as a team. I want to congratulate Belgium because they were good. But I think we were not at our level. 

"We started in a poor way. We never found the flow of the game. Even after a we scored, in the next action we conceded a goal. It was a very bad day. We didn't show the quality from previous games.

"We have to accept we weren't good. We don't need to find excuses.

"FIFA and all the different people involved explained, and those are the rules. It was a clear explanation, so I have no comment on that [President Trump admitting today he called Gianni Infantino to review Balogun's red].

"Belgium was the better team."





— https://nitter.net/JacobsBen/status/2074327779794157798#m

2074312772670906654.headline: Charles De Ketelaere
2074312772670906654.body: FT: USA 1 Belgium 4. Belgium comfortably into the QFs thanks to a Charles De Ketelaere's first-half brace, a Hans Vanaken goal from Matt Freese's error and a late injury time goal from Romelu Lukaku. A defensively poor display by the US with the main bright spark Malik Tillman's first-half deflected free-kick. US could only hold on at 1-1 for two minutes. So much momentum after winning their group, and credit to the players for that, but this was a flat way to bow out. Sebastian Berhalter came close to getting one back from long range, while Flo Balogun saw a powerful near-post shot saved by an untroubled Thibaut Courtois. Belgium never really tested. Rudi Garcia got his tactics spot on, while his players showed strong game management. Looks like the narrow win over Senegal from 2-0 down in the R32 was a wake up call for them.🇺🇸🇧🇪
2074312772670906654.raw_text:

Belgium never really tested. Rudi Garcia got his tactics spot on, while his players showed strong game management. Looks like the narrow win over Senegal from 2-0 down in the R32 was a wake up call for them.🇺🇸🇧🇪

2074424055923417157.headline: Mauricio Pochettino
2074424055923417157.body: U.S. national team head coach Mauricio Pochettino will wait on discussions over his future after the team’s elimination from the World Cup in the round of 16. He says “now is not the time” to talk about his role with the federation, after watching his side unravel to a Belgium side who now advance to a quarter-final against Spain. “In the next weeks, we can start to talk if the federation wants to talk,” Pochettino said. More from tombogert 🔗 nyti.ms/4w8hQE5

2074419517933883621.headline: When Ghana
2074419517933883621.body: The unsettling, unintentional story of the biggest soccer tournament in history and its expansion has been serious allegations of sexual assault around the globe — and football's aversion to saying anything about it. When Ghana head coach Carlos Queiroz criticised FIFA’s expansion of the World Cup to 48 teams as “ordinary” and “vulgar”, he probably didn’t realise how ironically and depressingly apt his choice of words were. nyti.ms/4f7G4H9

2074331846641430736.headline: Mauricio Pochettino
2074331846641430736.body: The irony is that, for months, Mauricio Pochettino had been making every effort to ensure politics did not become a distraction for his United States side at this World Cup. Then the US President Donald Trump sticks his oar in and the co-hosts go from being a picture of concentration to a lost rabble. We may never quite know the extent to which the noise, chaos and controversy surrounding the suspension of Folarin Balogun’s red card affected the USMNT players’ state of mind in those 36 hours before they took to the field against Belgium. But, watching the US disintegrate over the course of an agonising 90 plus minutes at Lumen Field in Seattle, it felt like Trump had achieved the opposite of what he probably set out to do: Pochettino’s carefully orchestrated approach to keep politics out of the football torpedoed amid a grotesque orgy of political narcissism and grandstanding. Full report from Seattle ⬇️ telegraph.co.uk/football/202… Link Trump favour backfires as US exits World Cup with limp Belgium performance President scored a monumental own goal as home team’s bid for glory in the round of 16 came to a shuddering, humiliating halt telegraph.co.uk

2074366575818240469.headline: World Cup
2074366575818240469.body: Fallout from USMNT World Cup exit 🔺Iran revel in US’ defeat. “Now the whole world is dancing to celebrate politics’ humiliating defeat by football.” 🔺 Belgium players appear to poke a little fun at Trump by mimicking US President’s signature dance 🔺Official Belgium X account says “Overturn this” in a sarcastic dig at the Balogun episode 🔺Rudi Garcia praises Balogun for coming to talk to him: “It’s not his fault, he’s not the one to blame, I told him that.” 🔺Balogun: “When you know you're given a red card, and then you know usually the protocol is you don’t play in the following game, and then when that decision's overturned, of course it’s going to be controversial” (He’s been so classy Balogun throughout this furore) 🔺Pochettino says he’ll rest a bit and then in “the next weeks” discuss his future with US Soccer, who have already presented a proposal to extend his contract until the end of the 2030 WC ⬇️ telegraph.co.uk/football/202… Link ‘The world is dancing’: Iran mock US exit as Belgium do the Trump jig Middle East nation gloat as co-hosts crash out of the World Cup despite Fifa’s controversial decision to suspend Folarin Balogun ban telegraph.co.uk

Fallout from #USMNT World Cup exit
🔺Iran revel in US’ defeat. “Now the whole world is dancing to celebrate politics’ humiliating defeat by football.”
🔺 #Belgium players appear to poke a little fun at Trump by mimicking US President’s signature dance
🔺Official Belgium X account says “Overturn this” in a sarcastic dig at the Balogun episode
🔺Rudi Garcia praises Balogun for coming to talk to him: “It’s not his fault, he’s not the one to blame, I told him that.”
🔺Balogun: “When you know you're given a red card, and then you know usually the protocol is you don’t play in the following game, and then when that decision's overturned, of course it’s going to be controversial” (He’s been so classy Balogun throughout this furore)
🔺Pochettino says he’ll rest a bit and then in “the next weeks” discuss his future with US Soccer, who have already presented a proposal to extend his contract until the end of the 2030 WC

2074298455661658330.headline: Pierluigi Collina
2074298455661658330.body: Fifa referees’ chief Pierluigi Collina has jumped to the defence of Raphael Claus after the official at the centre of the Folarin Balogun controversy was branded “a little bit suspect” by Trump. “Raphael Claus is refereeing at his second FIFA World Cup having been with us in Qatar in 2022. He is an experienced and highly respected referee and we maintain full confidence in him as a trusted match official.”
2074298455661658330.raw_text:
Fifa referees’ chief Pierluigi Collina has jumped to the defence of Raphael Claus after the official at the centre of the Folarin Balogun controversy was branded “a little bit suspect” by Trump.

“Raphael Claus is refereeing at his second FIFA World Cup having been with us in Qatar in 2022. He is an experienced and highly respected referee and we maintain full confidence in him as a trusted match official.”

2074475094957592750.headline: Jurgen Klopp
2074475094957592750.body: Jurgen Klopp and the German Football Association (DFB) will continue negotiations this weekend for the former Liverpool manager to become the national side’s next head coach. Klopp has already indicated his willingness to succeed Julian Nagelsmann, who resigned after Germany’s last-32 World Cup elimination by Paraguay, but is yet to finalise an agreement. More from SebSB & JamesPearceLFC 🔗 nyti.ms/4wuK7Ey
2074475094957592750.raw_text:
Jurgen Klopp and the German Football Association (DFB) will continue negotiations this weekend for the former Liverpool manager to become the national side’s next head coach.

Klopp has already indicated his willingness to succeed Julian Nagelsmann, who resigned after Germany’s last-32 World Cup elimination by Paraguay, but is yet to finalise an agreement.

More from @SebSB & @JamesPearceLFC

2074403293560746040.headline: Cristiano Ronaldo
2074403293560746040.body: Only eight men in history have scored more than Cristiano Ronaldo’s 11 World Cup goals. But relative to his brilliance, his longevity and his status in the sport, as the all-time record goalscorer in both club and international football, that is a frustratingly meagre return. For a sporting superhero, the World Cup has resembled kryptonite… But Ronaldo’s legacy and his story has been written over the past two decades. He should not spend the rest of his life fretting about the one that got away. OliverKay on Ronaldo, the World Cup and a question of legacy. 🔗 nytimes.com/athletic/7426532… Video
2074403293560746040.raw_text:
Only eight men in history have scored more than Cristiano Ronaldo’s 11 World Cup goals.

But relative to his brilliance, his longevity and his status in the sport, as the all-time record goalscorer in both club and international football, that is a frustratingly meagre return.

For a sporting superhero, the World Cup has resembled kryptonite…

But Ronaldo’s legacy and his story has been written over the past two decades. He should not spend the rest of his life fretting about the one that got away.

@OliverKay on Ronaldo, the World Cup and a question of legacy.

🔗 nytimes.com/athletic/7426532…

2074553657467826536.headline: Amadou Onana
2074553657467826536.body: 🚨 BREAKING: Belgium confirm Amadou Onana has ruptured his ACL. 🗣️ Team Doctor Brahim Hacene: “Unfortunately, the medical examinations have confirmed that Amadou has suffered a rupture of the anterior cruciate ligament (ACL). This is devastating news, both for him personally and for the team. “We immediately consulted with Amadou and with Aston Villa to agree on the best approach to his recovery. “Over the coming days, we will jointly determine the next steps in his medical treatment and rehabilitation. Throughout this process, we will continue to provide Amadou with our full support. In consultation with the player and Villa, it has been decided that Onana will remain with the squad at least until the end of the quarter-final.
2074553657467826536.raw_text:
🚨 BREAKING: Belgium confirm Amadou Onana has ruptured his ACL.

🗣️ Team Doctor Brahim Hacene: “Unfortunately, the medical examinations have confirmed that Amadou has suffered a rupture of the anterior cruciate ligament (ACL). This is devastating news, both for him personally and for the team.

“We immediately consulted with Amadou and with Aston Villa to agree on the best approach to his recovery. 

“Over the coming days, we will jointly determine the next steps in his medical treatment and rehabilitation. Throughout this process, we will continue to provide Amadou with our full support."

In consultation with the player and Villa, it has been decided that Onana will remain with the squad at least until the end of the quarter-final.

2074552941596508638.headline: Mauricio Pochettino
2074552941596508638.body: 🚨 US Soccer confirm positive conversations with Mauricio Pochettino about staying on. “We had positive conversations with Mauricio before the World Cup about the future. We agreed we would continue those conversations following a chance to rest and reflect post World Cup. We have a great deal of respect and gratitude for Mauricio, his staff and everyone part of the program. We have shared excitement about our potential and also shared clarity about the amount of work at all levels still required to achieve our ambition.”
2074552941596508638.raw_text:
🚨 US Soccer confirm positive conversations with Mauricio Pochettino about staying on.

“We had positive conversations with Mauricio before the World Cup about the future. We agreed we would continue those conversations following a chance to rest and reflect post World Cup. We have a great deal of respect and gratitude for Mauricio, his staff and everyone part of the program. We have shared excitement about our potential and also shared clarity about the amount of work at all levels still required to achieve our ambition.”

2074551746639392801.headline: Flo Balogun
2074551746639392801.body: 🗣️ RBFA statement confirms the case with FIFA remains “ongoing” following Flo Balogun’s suspended red card. “RBFA remains committed to advocating for a review of the current FIFA regulations and their application “The Royal Belgian Football Association (RBFA) is proud of the way the Red Devils responded on the pitch yesterday. “At the same time, the RBFA will continue to pursue the ongoing FIFA matter off the pitch. The RBFA firmly believes that international football is best served by a disciplinary and governance framework that fully upholds the principles of legal certainty, transparency, equal treatment and fair play. “Clear procedures, respect for the rights of defence and the consistent application of the regulations are essential to maintaining the confidence of member associations, coaches, players, supporters and all other stakeholders in our sport. “Regardless of the sporting outcome, the RBFA will continue to advocate for the correct and consistent application of these principles by FIFA, ensuring that any form of arbitrariness is avoided. “In doing so, we feel supported by millions of football fans around the world, as well as by many other member associations. “Finally, on behalf of the coaching staff and the players, we would like to sincerely thank the Belgian supporters for their incredible and unwavering support throughout this FIFA World Cup. Now, together, our full focus turns to Friday's quarter-final against Spain.”
2074551746639392801.raw_text:
🗣️ RBFA statement confirms the case with FIFA remains “ongoing” following Flo Balogun’s suspended red card.

“RBFA remains committed to advocating for a review of the current FIFA regulations and their application

“The Royal Belgian Football Association (RBFA) is proud of the way the Red Devils responded on the pitch yesterday.

“At the same time, the RBFA will continue to pursue the ongoing FIFA matter off the pitch. The RBFA firmly believes that international football is best served by a disciplinary and governance framework that fully upholds the principles of legal certainty, transparency, equal treatment and fair play.

“Clear procedures, respect for the rights of defence and the consistent application of the regulations are essential to maintaining the confidence of member associations, coaches, players, supporters and all other stakeholders in our sport.

“Regardless of the sporting outcome, the RBFA will continue to advocate for the correct and consistent application of these principles by FIFA, ensuring that any form of arbitrariness is avoided.

“In doing so, we feel supported by millions of football fans around the world, as well as by many other member associations.

“Finally, on behalf of the coaching staff and the players, we would like to sincerely thank the Belgian supporters for their incredible and unwavering support throughout this FIFA World Cup. Now, together, our full focus turns to Friday's quarter-final against Spain.”

2074555574537077156.headline: Leandro Paredes
2074555574537077156.body: Never in doubt for Argentina. 🤥 Moments after Leandro Paredes stopped a promising Egypt counter, Lautaro Martinez picked up the ball and surged down the wing before delivering a delightful cross that was headed home beautifully by Enzo Fernandez. The 2022 World Cup winners do not know when they are beaten. An impressive show of character from Argentina — goals from Cristian Romero, Lionel Messi and Fernandez's late, late header break Egypt hearts. They will face Switzerland or Colombia in the quarter-final.
2074555574537077156.raw_text:
Never in doubt for Argentina. 🤥

Moments after Leandro Paredes stopped a promising Egypt counter, Lautaro Martinez picked up the ball and surged down the wing before delivering a delightful cross that was headed home beautifully by Enzo Fernandez.

The 2022 World Cup winners do not know when they are beaten.

An impressive show of character from Argentina — goals from Cristian Romero, Lionel Messi and Fernandez's late, late header break Egypt hearts. 

They will face Switzerland or Colombia in the quarter-final.

The exciting young striker was on loan at Huddersfield Town last season before his stint in Yorkshire was cut short by injury. Redmond signed for Villa last summer, joining from Feyenoord. He played for the Dutch giants in the Champions League and has also represented the Netherlands at junior level.
2074538944973492567.raw_text:
Aston Villa can confirm that Zepiqueno Redmond has joined Annecy on a season-long loan.

The exciting young striker was on loan at Huddersfield Town last season before his stint in Yorkshire was cut short by injury.

Redmond signed for Villa last summer, joining from Feyenoord. He played for the Dutch giants in the Champions League and has also represented the Netherlands at junior level.

2074817226226479288.headline: Zlatko Dalic
2074817226226479288.body: Croatia manager Zlatko Dalic has stepped down after the national team’s World Cup exit in the round-of-32 stage. After his appointment in October 2017, Dalic led Croatia to the 2018 World Cup final against France. Four years ago in Qatar, Croatia followed up their 2018 run with a third-place finish, losing 3-0 to eventual winners France in the semi-finals having knocked Brazil in the last-eight stage. 🔗 nyti.ms/44ecNFJ
2074817226226479288.raw_text:
Croatia manager Zlatko Dalic has stepped down after the national team’s World Cup exit in the round-of-32 stage.

After his appointment in October 2017, Dalic led Croatia to the 2018 World Cup final against France.

Four years ago in Qatar, Croatia followed up their 2018 run with a third-place finish, losing 3-0 to eventual winners France in the semi-finals having knocked Brazil in the last-eight stage.

2074905020630544406.headline: Bruno Guimarães
2074905020630544406.body: Arsenal pursuit of Bruno Guimarães ongoing. Although Newcastle insist no direct contact from Arsenal yet, a new bid is expected of around £60m. Arsenal informed via intermediaries last month that Newcastle don't want to sell, but Guimarães is keen on the move.🇧🇷 Ben Jacobs (JacobsBen) Arsenal prepared to make a formal bid to Newcastle for Bruno Guimaraes after seeing a verbal offer via intermediaries of around £55m turned down. Newcastle insist they don’t wish to sell, but direct club-to-club contact is now anticipated. As reported yesterday, Alex Scott, Ayyoub Bouaddi and Sandro Tonali other names on the radar. Matheus Fernandes has also been looked at, but Spurs and Manchester United more advanced as it stands. Overlap with other Premier League clubs for each target. MCFC want another midfielder post Elliot Anderson. Spurs also want to sign two midfielders. Manchester United will add at least one more midfielder. And Chelsea and Liverpool could also explore a central midfield reinforcement. More on talkSPORT.🎙️ 🤝 alex_crook —
2074905020630544406.raw_text:
Arsenal pursuit of Bruno Guimarães ongoing. Although Newcastle insist no direct contact from Arsenal yet, a new bid is expected of around £60m.

Arsenal informed via intermediaries last month that Newcastle don't want to sell, but Guimarães is keen on the move.🇧🇷


Ben Jacobs (@JacobsBen)

Arsenal prepared to make a formal bid to Newcastle for Bruno Guimaraes after seeing a verbal offer via intermediaries of around £55m turned down. Newcastle insist they don’t wish to sell, but direct club-to-club contact is now anticipated.

As reported yesterday, Alex Scott, Ayyoub Bouaddi and Sandro Tonali other names on the radar. Matheus Fernandes has also been looked at, but Spurs and Manchester United more advanced as it stands.

Overlap with other Premier League clubs for each target. #MCFC want another midfielder post Elliot Anderson.

Spurs also want to sign two midfielders.

Manchester United will add at least one more midfielder.

And Chelsea and Liverpool could also explore a central midfield reinforcement.

More on @talkSPORT.🎙️

deferred.headline: Atletico Madrid
deferred.body: Atletico Madrid is being assessed and the club is monitoring the situation.
deferred.text:
⏳🚨 Bayer 04 Leverkusen are willing to sell Alejandro #Grimaldo and are already working on a replacement.
Initial asking price is €30m as reported. An agreement from around €25m upwards cannot be ruled out.
Leverkusen do not want to block the move. Grimaldo wants to leave and already has a full agreement in place with Atletico Madrid.
Negotiations between the clubs are ongoing.
 🇪🇸

deferred.headline: Jones
deferred.body: Jones is set to stay at Liverpool; no exit is planned at this stage.
deferred.text:
Inter made a fresh approach for Curtis Jones last week asking whether a deal was possible for around €25m.
This was a conversation not a formal or written offer.
Liverpool made it clear no sale at this price, especially given numbers other midfielders are expected to be sold for this summer.
Liverpool are prepared to keep Jones unless a suitable bid arises, even if that means losing him on a free next summer.
Liverpool have never had concerns about Jones' attitude or professionalism despite Inter reapproaching the club, and growing interest elsewhere.
Understand the club's position has hardened as a result of Inter's latest contact. If the right offer emerges, a sale will be sanctioned, but Inter would have to significantly increase their valuation to get Liverpool to engage.
Worth noting, Liverpool are also comfortable with Jones staying, and if that happens, and he impresses Andoni Iraola, they could yet decide to engage in new contract talks.🔴
deferred.draft_caption:
📝 REPORTED: Jones (Liverpool)
Jones is set to stay at Liverpool; no exit is planned at this stage.
